reasoning_model_1: The model fails to adhere to the core requirements of the prompt on multiple levels. First, regarding Content Accuracy, the model hallucinates a repetition loop, repeating the target sentence '快看，那是什么？一闪而过！' five times continuously from 00:00 to 00:10, violating the implied instruction to speak the sentence once. Second, regarding the target feature '语速' (Speech Speed), the pacing is not 'extremely fast' as requested. The timestamps indicate each repetition takes approximately 2 seconds (e.g., 00:00 to 00:02) for roughly 11 characters, which corresponds to a normal speaking rate (~5.5 characters/second). The model fails to generate the required paralinguistic feature and produces an unnatural, repetitive output.
reasoning_model_2: The model successfully generates an audio clip that is highly compliant with the target feature '语速' (Speech Speed). The timestamp indicates the speech starts at 00:00 and concludes within a very short timeframe (implied <2 seconds given the single line), suggesting a high character-per-second rate (~9-10 chars/sec), effectively capturing the 'extremely fast' and urgent nature of the prompt. However, there is a minor Content Accuracy issue: the transcript shows the model said '看看' (Look look) instead of the scripted '快看' (Quick look). While this slightly deviates from the text, the core meaning and the primary objective of extreme speed are well-preserved.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 exhibits a critical failure in generation control, entering a repetition loop that lasts 10 seconds and fails to achieve the requested 'extremely fast' speed, delivering the text at a normal pace instead. Model 2, conversely, successfully executes the 'extremely fast' delivery, creating a sense of urgency. Although Model 2 has a minor pronunciation/content deviation ('看看' vs '快看'), it is far superior to Model 1's complete structural and feature failure.
